options: Both will turn blue-black because starch is present in both.
Tube A will turn blue-black, Tube B will not, as boiling activates enzymes.
Tube A will remain the colour of iodine, Tube B will turn blue-black, because boiling denatures salivary amylase.
Neither will change colour as saliva neutralizes iodine.
questionText: Consider two test tubes: Tube A contains starch solution + saliva. Tube B contains starch solution + boiled saliva. After 30 minutes, dilute iodine is added to both. What will be the colour change, and why?
